mnemonics.title: PMF vs PDF
mnemonics.content: PMF = Mass (discrete, has weight at points), PDF = Density (continuous, area under curve)

mnemonics.title: Binomial Conditions
mnemonics.content: BINOMIAL: B - Binary outcomes, I - Independent trials, N - Number fixed, O - Outcome counted, M - Multiplied probabilities, I - Individual p, A - Add across k, L - Like binomial coefficients

acronyms.usage: For discrete random variables - assigns probability to each point
M_X(t) = E[e^(tX)] - used to find moments
Each trial has same probability and doesn't affect others

visualTips.visualization: Discrete PMF looks like vertical bars at integer values with heights = probabilities; bars don't touch (unlike continuous PDF)
CDF is a staircase that jumps up at each possible value; right-continuous so steps include the endpoint
Imagine data points around mean; variance is average squared distance from mean - wider spread = larger variance

analogies.analogy: The 'center of gravity' of the probability distribution - where the 'seesaw' would balance
Like counting lightning strikes in a year or typos on a page - rare events in a large population/interval
Like waiting for a bus that comes with probability p each minute - how many minutes until it arrives?
If you've waited 10 minutes for a bus (geometric), waiting 5 more minutes has same probability of arrival as waiting from scratch
